target.title: fix(agents): allow compaction config tuning
target.body:
## Summary

  - Problem: agent-driven `gateway config.patch` / `gateway config.apply` rejected documented compaction
  budget and notification settings.
  - Why it matters: operators could not safely tune compaction behavior through the gateway tool path even
  though these fields are intended configuration knobs.
  - What changed: allowlisted only the documented compaction tuning keys and added guard coverage for both
  `config.patch` and `config.apply`.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): neighboring compaction policy fields such as provider/model
  selection, truncation policy, memory flush, and identifier instructions remain protected.

  ## Change Type (select all)

  - [x] Bug fix
  - [ ] Feature
  - [ ] Refactor required for the fix
  - [x] Docs
  - [ ] Security hardening
  - [ ] Chore/infra

  ## Scope (select all touched areas)

  - [x] Gateway / orchestration
  - [x] Skills / tool execution
  - [ ] Auth / tokens
  - [ ] Memory / storage
  - [ ] Integrations
  - [x] API / contracts
  - [ ] UI / DX
  - [ ] CI/CD / infra

  ## Linked Issue/PR

  - Closes #78911
  - Related #
  - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

  ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

  - Behavior or issue addressed: agent-driven gateway config updates can now tune documented compaction
  budget/notice fields while still rejecting adjacent protected policy fields.
  - Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS.
  - Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-
  coverage.test.ts`; `pnpm build`; docs checks listed below.
  - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log,
  linked artifact, or copied live output): targeted guard test passed with 44 tests; `pnpm build` passed;
  `pnpm docs:check-mdx` passed; `pnpm docs:check-links` passed.
  - Observed result after fix: allowed compaction tuning keys pass the gateway tool guard for `config.patch`
  and `config.apply`; protected neighboring fields remain blocked by test coverage.
  - What was not tested: broad Testbox changed gate; `blacksmith` was unavailable locally.
  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing guard behavior did not include these compaction keys
  in the allowed config path set.

  ## Root Cause (if applicable)

  - Root cause: the gateway config write guard allowlist did not include the documented
  `agents.defaults.compaction.*` tuning fields.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: coverage did not assert that documented compaction tuning fields were
  writable through `config.patch` / `config.apply`.
  - Contributing context (if known): the guard correctly failed closed, but the allowlist was too narrow for
  this documented configuration surface.

  ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - Coverage level that should have caught this:
    - [x] Unit test
    - [x] Seam / integration test
    - [ ] End-to-end test
    - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts`
  - Scenario the test should lock in: `config.patch` and `config.apply` allow only documented compaction
  tuning fields and reject adjacent policy fields.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in the gateway tool config guard allowlist, so
  direct guard coverage catches the behavior without involving unrelated gateway runtime setup.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for these specific compaction keys.
  - If no new test is added, why not: N/A

  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  Agent-driven gateway config updates can now tune these documented fields:

  - `agents.defaults.compaction.keepRecentTokens`
  - `agents.defaults.compaction.maxHistoryShare`
  - `agents.defaults.compaction.maxActiveTranscriptBytes`
  - `agents.defaults.compaction.recentTurnsPreserve`
  - `agents.defaults.compaction.notifyUser`

  ## Diagram (if applicable)

  ```text
  Before:
  [agent config.patch compaction tuning] -> [gateway guard rejects documented field]

  After:
  [agent config.patch compaction tuning] -> [gateway guard allows documented tuning field] -> [protected
  neighboring policy fields still rejected]
  ```

  ## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (Yes/No) Yes
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No) No
  - New/changed network calls? (Yes/No) No
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No) Yes
  - Data access scope changed? (Yes/No) No
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: this expands agent-driven config writes only for five documented
    compaction tuning fields. Risk is limited by exact path allowlisting and negative tests that keep
    adjacent policy fields protected.

  ## Repro + Verification

  ### Environment

  - OS: macOS
  - Runtime/container: Node/pnpm local checkout
  - Model/provider: N/A
  - Integration/channel (if any): gateway tool config guard
  - Relevant config (redacted): N/A

  ### Steps

  1. Run pnpm test src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts.
  2. Run pnpm docs:check-mdx.
  3. Run pnpm docs:check-links.
  4. Run pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/gateway-tool.ts src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-
     guard-coverage.test.ts.
  5. Run git diff --check.
  6. Run pnpm build.

  ### Expected

  - Documented compaction tuning keys are accepted for agent-driven config.patch and config.apply.
  - Neighboring compaction policy fields remain rejected.
  - Docs and build checks pass.

  ### Actual

  - Targeted guard test passed.
  - Docs checks passed.
  - Formatting check passed.
  - git diff --check passed.
  - pnpm build passed.

  ## Evidence

  Attach at least one:

  - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
  - [ ] Trace/log snippets
  - [ ] Screenshot/recording
  - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

  ## Human Verification (required)

  What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

  - Verified scenarios: allowed compaction tuning keys for config.patch; allowed compaction tuning keys for
    config.apply; protected neighboring compaction fields remain rejected.
  - Edge cases checked: provider/model policy fields, truncation policy, memory flush, and identifier
    instruction fields are still blocked.
  - What you did not verify: broad Testbox changed gate because blacksmith was unavailable locally.

  ## Review Conversations

  - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

  If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot
  review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

  ## Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? (Yes/No) Yes
  - Config/env changes? (Yes/No) No
  - Migration needed? (Yes/No) No
  - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

  ## Risks and Mitigations

  - Risk: expanding agent-driven config writes could accidentally expose protected compaction policy
    controls.
      - Mitigation: only exact documented tuning paths were allowlisted, with regression tests proving
        adjacent policy fields remain protected.

### Built with GPT 5.5 xHigh
target.author: sahilsatralkar
